goal: Split structural.packages (Robert C. Martin D') into structural.packages.rigidity + structural.packages.uselessness — each names a distinct failure mode off the main sequence

summary.title: Split structural.packages → .rigidity + .uselessness (Martin 1994)
summary.what: Robert C. Martin's D' (Distance from the Main Sequence) names a single metric but two distinct failures off the main sequence: Pain (stable + concrete = rigid) or Uselessness (unstable + abstract = wasted indirection). Split structural.packages into .rigidity and .uselessness, each filtering robert_kernel results by zone before threshold-checking D'. Same kernel drives both. fail_on_zone param removed — the rule name names the zone. _compat additions migrate v2.0 [rules.structural.packages] tables and the legacy [rules.packages] alias to the new layout (the v2.0 default fail_on_zone=['pain'] maps to .rigidity to preserve observed behaviour). 501 tests pass (+7 new: pass-when-clean × 2, skip-unsupported × 2, zone isolation × 3 via monkeypatched kernel, default-config × 2, legacy migration × 1).
summary.why: D' alone is descriptive (off the main sequence) but not prescriptive. Naming the zone in the rule name surfaces the failure mode directly in finder output and waiver references: 'rigidity' tells you to introduce abstractions, 'uselessness' tells you to collapse them. Per-rule thresholds also let users tune sensitivity per failure mode (e.g. tolerate abstract scaffolding while flagging rigidity early).
summary.user_goal: Split structural.packages (Robert C. Martin D') into structural.packages.rigidity + structural.packages.uselessness — each names a distinct failure mode off the main sequence

decisions.chose: Two rules sharing one kernel (filter by zone in rule layer) rather than one parameterised rule. Naming the zone in the rule name surfaces the failure mode in finder output and waiver references.
Default threshold 0.7 on each rule (preserving v2.0 max_distance default). Users tune independently.
structural.packages alias maps to .rigidity (not both) — v2.0 default fail_on_zone was ['pain'], so observed user behaviour was rigidity-only. Mapping to both would silently change defaults.

Hand-rolled _migrate_v21_packages_split rather than extending LEGACY_TABLE_MIGRATIONS to handle nested-dotted-path keys. Symmetric with _migrate_v110_stutter_subrules; the more general mechanism can wait for a second use case.
Kept robert_kernel unchanged. Rule layer is the only place that fans out.
